rationale: Novemsky et al. (2007) is not only listed as background; it is cited as prior evidence for negative effects of metacognitive difficulty in consumer choice and is specifically referenced in the method as a precedent for the difficult-to-read font manipulation. The citing paper then positions its own contribution relative to that stream by showing a domain-based reversal in evaluation outcomes and discussing implications for Novemsky et al.'s deferral finding. Because the citation helps frame the theoretical baseline and informs design choices, its role is substantive.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on the preference-fluency line represented by Novemsky et al. (2007), using similar fluency and attribution logic while shifting to product evaluation and willingness-to-pay outcomes. It adds consumption domain (everyday vs special occasion) as a moderator, arguing that metacognitive difficulty can reverse valence in special domains.
